name: Braised Squab in a Mold of Vegetables
recipeInstructions:
Squabs and Sauce:
1. Brown pigeon wings and neck in the oven and reserve.
2. Salt and pepper the squab inside and out.
3. In a large saute pan, heat butter. Add the squabs and brown carefully on every side.
4. Place the birds in a warm spot and discard used butter from the pan.
5. Add 4 tablespoons of fresh clarified butter to the pan.
6. Add bacon and onions to the pan and saute to a blond color.
7. Add cabbages and cook over low heat for twenty minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Add veal stock to cover the contents of the pan and adjust seasonings. Bring to a boil.
9. Place the squabs on the cabbage in the saute pan and brush with clarified butter. Cook in a 450 F oven for 10 minutes.
10. Remove the squabs from the pan, bone them and put bones in the saucepan with previously cut wings and necks. Reserve the squab meat.
11. Strain the juice from the cabbage mixture into the saucepan with the necks, wings, and bones. Add veal stock if liquid is needed. Reserve the cabbage.
12. Over medium heat, cook strained cabbage juice and bones for at least one-half hour. Strain through a chinois and reserve the sauce.
Vegetables and Molds:
1. Cook carrots, turnips, and broccoli in heavily salted water until crisp. Cool vegetables in an ice water bowl.
2. Select the largest turnip, slice into 4 slices about 1/8-inch thick and 3 1/2 inches in diameter. Cut a round hole about the size of a quarter or slightly larger in each turnip slice.
3. Cut remaining turnips and carrots into 40 rectangular slices (1 1/2 inch by 1 inch by 1/8 inch).
4. Place one large turnip slice on the bottom of each buttered 4-inch souffle mold. Arrange remaining vegetable slices around the inside edge of molds, alternating carrot and turnip slices.
5. Place a broccoli flower (stem off) into the hole in the bottom of each mold. This will be the centerpiece when unmolded later.
6. Cover broccoli with one layer of cabbage packed well over and between the vegetables.
7. Top cabbage layer with another layer of squab meat.
8. Add more cabbage, pressing hard to form the mixture at the top of the mold.
9. Place molds on a small sheet pan. Surround molds with a little water (1/4 inch depth or slightly more). Put buttered parchment paper on each mold (butter side down).
10. Reheat in the oven at 450 F for 20 minutes.
11. Unmold onto serving plates. Spoon warmed sauce around molds and serve.
